question: B-001-retrieval-decay
claim: Emit a coarse score_band (high/med/low) relative to THIS recall's top hit (not a global confidence) so token-thrifty callers gauge near-top without parsing raw fused scores.
evidence: retrieval.md RRF section; formatters/search-results.ts:144 (scoreResolution enum); content-router.ts:1144 (confidenceBandFor exists for routing not recall)

question: B-001-retrieval-decay
claim: Lexical exact-anchor guard: an EXACT surface match over top BM25 hits gets a visible fused contribution so a broad query can't bury a precise memory under weak semantic re-ranks. A fusion signal, not a query-class profile (distinct from C2).

question: B-001-retrieval-decay
claim: Competition ranking (equal scores share a rank) so a uniform re-rank field (decay-off, uniform importance) contributes a constant and reorders nothing — correctness fix for the rank-fusion.

question: B-001-retrieval-decay
claim: Four decay short-circuits: pinned; inert half-life (non-finite/non-positive=no-decay); non-finite importance (never mint NaN); non-positive elapsed (future last_access clamps to 0 so clock regression can't INFLATE importance).

question: B-001-retrieval-decay
claim: Pin short-circuits INSIDE the decay function (keeps full write-time importance in EVERY ranking). Internally is_pinned only governs tier(HOT)+retention, not rank-time importance.

question: B-001-retrieval-decay
claim: Decay reads last_access (staleness of USE); recency orders by ingestion instant (newness of RECORD) — different time columns, must not double-count.

question: B-001-retrieval-decay
claim: forget_eligible = strict AND over pure axes (unpinned AND decayed-importance<floor AND trust<floor AND unreferenced AND >=min-age); any single axis can only SPARE, never doom; sweep reads decay through the SAME functions as rank-time.

question: B-001-retrieval-decay
claim: Recall-time `sensitive` flag narrows EVERY fact signal to provenance-grounded rows (support set chosen once at top of run). Distinct from C8 (output escaping); this is recall-time content gating. Internally provenance exists only at INGEST.

question: B-001-retrieval-decay
claim: Memory retrieval/decay is largely SATURATED vs the roadmap (retrieval.md maps onto C2-C9/C-X1; decay onto C6-A). Net-new is 8 mostly-FIX hardening candidates; the competition-rank + decay-guards + pin-at-rank + dual-axis cluster is a coherent small sub-packet. newInfoRatio 0.35.
